seoTitle: How to Add a Timeline Slicer in Power BI to Filter Dates
metaDescription: Learn how to add a timeline slicer in Power BI so viewers can filter reports by date range visually, making trend analysis faster and clearer.
intro: Standard dropdown filters work fine for dates, but a visual timeline makes it much easier for users to explore trends across a specific period. This guide shows how to add a timeline slicer in Power BI so date filtering becomes an interactive, visual experience. It's especially useful for executives and analysts who want to isolate seasonal patterns or specific reporting periods quickly.

steps.heading: Add a timeline slicer to your report
steps.body: Insert a slicer visual and format it, or use a timeline-style custom visual, connecting it to your date field so it displays a visual date range.

steps.heading: Configure date ranges
steps.body: Set the initial range and granularity, such as by month or quarter, so the timeline defaults to a view that makes sense for your report.

steps.heading: Visualize data with the timeline slicer
steps.body: Confirm that other visuals on the page respond correctly as users adjust the timeline, filtering the report to the selected date range.

steps.heading: Apply key use cases
steps.body: Use the timeline slicer for scenarios like comparing seasonal sales patterns or reviewing performance across a specific quarter without manually setting start and end dates each time.

faq.question: Is the timeline slicer different from a standard date slicer in Power BI?
faq.answer: Yes, a timeline slicer typically shows a continuous visual bar representing the date range, making it more intuitive for range selection than a basic list or dropdown.

faq.question: Can I set the timeline slicer to default to the last 30 days?
faq.answer: Yes, you can configure a default selection or use relative date filtering so the timeline opens already focused on a recent period.

faq.question: Does the timeline slicer affect every visual on the page?
faq.answer: By default it filters all visuals on the same page that use the connected date field, though this can be adjusted through slicer interaction settings.

faq.question: Can I sync a timeline slicer across multiple report pages?
faq.answer: Yes, using the Sync Slicers feature, you can apply the same timeline selection across several pages in the report.
